---
title: "Behavioural economics resources"
title-block-style: none
pagetitle: "Behavioural economics resources | Mesfin Genie"
description: "Public teaching resources for behavioural economics."
page-layout: full
image: /assets/img/og-card.png
---

```{=html}
<header class="page-header"><div class="page-shell"><p class="eyebrow">Behavioural economics resources</p><h1>Benchmarks first, behavioural mechanisms second</h1><p class="lead">These teaching notes translate selected ECON3111 materials into public learning resources. They emphasise clear benchmarks, real-world motivation, mathematical notation, worked examples, learning activities and exam-relevant synthesis.</p></div></header><section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell grid-2"><article class="resource-card"><img src="/assets/teaching/week1-foundations.webp" width="900" height="506" alt="Week 1 slide thumbnail"><h2>Foundations and rational choice under certainty</h2><p>Based on Week 1 material: descriptive versus normative claims, rational choice as a benchmark, preferences as relations, completeness, transitivity, utility representation, budget sets and why behavioural economics studies systematic deviations from the benchmark.</p><p><a href="/blog/posts/the-benchmark-before-bias/">Related blog post →</a></p></article><article class="resource-card"><img src="/assets/teaching/week6-risk-uncertainty.webp" width="900" height="506" alt="Week 6 slide thumbnail"><h2>Advanced choice under risk and uncertainty</h2><p>Based on Week 6 material: expected utility, independence, the Allais and Ellsberg paradoxes, probability weighting, prospect theory, loss aversion, ambiguity aversion and multiple-priors reasoning.</p><p><a href="/blog/posts/why-99-does-not-feel-like-100/">Related blog post →</a></p></article></div></section>
```
